input.prompt:
Explore this monorepo codebase thoroughly. I need to understand:

1. The overall directory structure (apps/web, apps/desktop, apps/server, packages/shared, packages/contracts)
2. In packages/shared: what files exist, how exports are structured (subpath exports), look at package.json for export patterns
3. In apps/web: the React app structure, how components are organized, any existing context/provider patterns, the main App component, how CSS/styling is done, the entry point
4. Any existing theme or styling patterns
5. TypeScript conventions used (interfaces vs types, naming patterns)
6. Look at tsconfig files for path aliases

Be very thorough - read key files fully including package.json files, tsconfig files, the main App component, any existing providers/contexts, and the entry point files.

Name: `@t3tools/shared` - Version: `0.0.0-alpha.1` (private) - Type: ES modules **Export Pattern** (Subpath Exports): ```json "exports": { "./model": { "types": "./src/model.ts", "import": "./src/model.ts" }, "./git": { "types": "./src/git.ts", "import": "./src/git.ts" }, "./logging": { "types": "./src/logging.ts", "import": "./src/logging.ts" }, "./shell": { "types": "./src/shell.ts", "import": "./src/shell.ts" }, "./Net": { "types": "./src/Net.ts", "import": "./src/Net.ts" } } ``` **Files**: - `model.ts` - Model slug normalization and provider model handling - `logging.ts` - RotatingFileSink class for log file rotation (file I/O utilities) - `git.ts` - Git integration helpers - `shell.ts` - Shell execution utilities - `Net.ts` - Network utilities **Key Technologies**: - Effect (from catalog) - Functional programming library - Vitest for testing - TypeScript with Effect language service --- ### 3. packages/contracts Directory **Purpose**: Shared type definitions and API contracts used across all applications **Package Structure** (`/packages/contracts/package.json`): - Name: `@t3tools/contracts` - Version: `0.0.8` (private) - Build Tool: tsdown (compiles to both ESM and CJS) - Exports root with both CJS and ESM formats **Files** (Type Definitions): - `baseSchemas.ts` - Branded entity IDs using Effect Schema: - ThreadId, ProjectId, CommandId, EventId, MessageId, TurnId - ProviderItemId, RuntimeSessionId, RuntimeItemId, RuntimeRequestId, RuntimeTaskId - ApprovalRequestId, CheckpointRef - Uses branded string types with trim + non-empty validation - `orchestration.ts` - Large orchestration contracts - `provider.ts` - Provider contracts - `providerRuntime.ts` - Runtime provider contracts - `git.ts` - Git contracts - `editor.ts` - Editor contracts - `project.ts` - Project contracts **Type Convention**: Uses Effect Schema for branded types and validation --- ### 4. apps/web Directory - React App Structure **Purpose**: Main React web application UI **Technology Stack** (`/apps/web/package.json`): - **UI Framework**: React 19.0.0 - **Router**: TanStack React Router 1.160.2 (with plugin for auto-generated routes) - **State Management**: Zustand 5.0.11 (lightweight Zustand stores) - **Styling**: Tailwind CSS 4.0.0 with @tailwindcss/vite plugin - **Build Tool**: Vite 8.0.0-beta.12 - **Components**: - @base-ui/react 1.2.0 (Base UI primitives) - class-variance-authority 0.7.1 (CVA for variant utilities) - lucide-react 0.564.0 (icon library) - **Data Fetching**: @tanstack/react-query 5.90.0 - **Rich Text Editing**: @lexical/react 0.41.0, lexical 0.41.0 - **Terminal**: @xterm/xterm 6.0.0, @xterm/addon-fit - **Utilities**: - tailwind-merge 3.4.0 (merge Tailwind classes) - react-markdown 10.1.0 - @pierre/diffs (diff visualization) - @tanstack/react-pacer (timeline pacing) - @tanstack/react-virtual (virtualization) - **Build Optimization**: Babel React Compiler (beta) - **Testing**: Vitest, @vitest/browser-playwright - **MSW**: Mock Service Worker for testing #### Entry Point & Router **Main Entry** (`/apps/web/src/main.tsx`): ```tsx import React from "react"; import ReactDOM from "react-dom/client"; import { RouterProvider } from "@tanstack/react-router"; import { createHashHistory, createBrowserHistory } from "@tanstack/react-router"; import { isElectron } from "./env"; import { getRouter } from "./router"; import { APP_DISPLAY_NAME } from "./branding"; const history = isElectron ? createHashHistory() : createBrowserHistory(); const router = getRouter(history); document.title = APP_DISPLAY_NAME; ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root") as HTMLElement).render( , ); ``` **HTML Entry** (`/apps/web/index.html`): - Mounts to `
` - Uses Google Fonts (DM Sans font family) - Links to favicon and apple-touch-icon **Router Setup** (`/apps/web/src/router.ts`): - Creates QueryClient (TanStack React Query) - Wraps app with: 1. QueryClientProvider (React Query) 2. StoreProvider (Zustand store provider) - Routes defined in `routeTree.gen.ts` (auto-generated) #### Route Structure Routes are in `/apps/web/src/routes/`: - `__root.tsx` - Root layout component - Contains EventRouter for WebSocket event handling - Contains DesktopProjectBootstrap for Electron bootstrap - Provides ToastProvider and AnchoredToastProvider - Root error boundary with custom error UI - `_chat.tsx` - Chat layout wrapper - `_chat.index.tsx` - Chat index/default route - `_chat.$threadId.tsx` - Individual thread view - `_chat.settings.tsx` - Settings page #### Root Route Component (`__root.tsx`) Key Features: - **Event Router**: Subscribes to orchestration domain events and syncs server state - **WebSocket Integration**: - Listens to terminal events - Listens to domain events (thread.turn-diff-completed, thread.reverted) - Validates snapshot sequences to prevent out-of-order updates - **Query Invalidation**: On certain events, invalidates provider queries - **Bootstrap Handling**: Navigates to bootstrapped thread if provided - **Keybinding Updates**: Monitors server config updates for invalid keybindings - **Error Boundary**: Shows styled error UI with expandable details - **Connection State**: Shows "Connecting to server..." message before NativeApi ready #### Directory Structure ``` /apps/web/src/ ├── components/ │ ├── ui/ # Base UI components (from shadcn/base-ui) │ │ ├── button.tsx │ │ ├── card.tsx │ │ ├── input-group.tsx │ │ ├── popover.tsx │ │ ├── sheet.tsx │ │ ├── field.tsx │ │ ├── scroll-area.tsx │ │ ├── label.tsx │ │ ├── alert-dialog.tsx │ │ ├── tooltip.tsx │ │ └── [many more UI primitives] │ ├── BranchToolbar.tsx │ ├── BranchToolbarBranchSelector.tsx │ ├── ChatView.tsx (213KB - large component) │ ├── ChatView.browser.tsx │ ├── ChatMarkdown.tsx │ ├── ComposerPromptEditor.tsx │ ├── DiffPanel.tsx │ ├── DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx │ ├── GitActionsControl.tsx │ ├── Icons.tsx │ ├── PlanSidebar.tsx │ ├── ProjectScriptsControl.tsx │ ├── Sidebar.tsx │ ├── ThreadTerminalDrawer.tsx │ └── [+ test files and logic files] ├── hooks/ │ ├── useTheme.ts # Custom theme hook │ ├── useMediaQuery.ts │ └── useTurnDiffSummaries.ts ├── lib/ │ ├── utils.ts # cn() utility, platform detection, ID generators │ ├── projectReactQuery.ts │ ├── serverReactQuery.ts │ ├── providerReactQuery.ts │ ├── gitReactQuery.ts │ ├── projectScriptKeybindings.ts │ ├── diffRendering.ts │ ├── turnDiffTree.ts │ ├── lruCache.ts │ └── [+ test files] ├── routes/ │ ├── __root.tsx │ ├── _chat.tsx │ ├── _chat.index.tsx │ ├── _chat.$threadId.tsx │ └── _chat.settings.tsx ├── store.ts # Main Zustand store ├── composerDraftStore.ts # Composer draft state (Zustand) ├── terminalStateStore.ts # Terminal state management (Zustand) ├── types.ts # App-level type definitions ├── main.tsx # Entry point ├── index.css # Global Tailwind styles ├── router.ts # Router configuration ├── keybindings.ts # Keybinding logic ├── appSettings.ts # Application settings ├── nativeApi.ts # Native API initialization ├── wsNativeApi.ts # WebSocket Native API wrapper ├── wsTransport.ts # WebSocket transport ├── vscode-icons.ts # VS Code icon handling ├── branding.ts # App branding constants ├── env.ts # Environment detection └── [many other utility files and tests] ``` --- ### 5. Existing Theme & Styling Patterns #### Theme Hook (`/apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`) Implements a custom theme system: - **Theme Options**: `"light" | "dark" | "system"` - **Storage Key**: `"t3code:theme"` (localStorage) - **Media Query**: `"(prefers-color-scheme: dark)"` - **Implementation**: Uses `useSyncExternalStore` for external state subscription - **Features**: - Applies theme to document.documentElement with `.dark` class - Suppresses transitions during theme changes to avoid flashing - Listens to system preference changes - Syncs theme changes across tabs via storage events - Returns `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }` #### CSS Architecture (`/apps/web/src/index.css`) **Uses Tailwind CSS 4.0 with:** - Custom `dark` variant: `&:is(.dark, .dark *)` - Inline theme configuration with CSS custom properties: - Color variables (primary, secondary, accent, destructive, etc.) - Radius scales (sm, md, lg, xl, 2xl, 3xl, 4xl) - Custom animations (skeleton) - **Color Scheme**: Light and dark mode CSS variables - **Root Colors** (Light): - Primary: oklch(0.488 0.217 264) - Purple/blue - Background: white - Foreground: neutral-800 - Borders using alpha values - **Dark Mode Variant**: Adjusts all colors for dark theme - **Base Layers**: Default border and outline styling applied globally - **No-transitions Class**: Suppresses transitions for smooth theme switching #### UI Component Pattern **Button Component Example** (`/apps/web/src/components/ui/button.tsx`): ```tsx import { mergeProps } from "@base-ui/react/merge-props"; import { useRender } from "@base-ui/react/use-render"; import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"; import { cn } from "~/lib/utils"; // CVA for variant styling const buttonVariants = cva( "[base classes with Tailwind + complex selectors]", { defaultVariants: { size: "default", variant: "default" }, variants: { size: { /* size options */ }, variant: { /* visual variants */ }, }, }, ); interface ButtonProps extends useRender.ComponentProps<"button"> { variant?: VariantProps["variant"]; size?: VariantProps["size"]; } function Button({ className, variant, size, render, ...props }: ButtonProps) { const defaultProps = { className: cn(buttonVariants({ className, size, variant })), "data-slot": "button", type: render ? undefined : "button", }; return useRender({ defaultTagName: "button", props: mergeProps<"button">(defaultProps, props), render, }); } ``` **Key Pattern**: - Uses @base-ui/react for headless UI primitives - Uses class-variance-authority (CVA) for variant management - Uses `cn()` utility from `~/lib/utils` to merge Tailwind classes - Uses `useRender` hook from Base UI for polymorphic rendering - Uses `mergeProps` for proper prop merging #### components.json Configuration ```json { "style": "base-mira", "rsc": false, "tsx": true, "tailwind": { "config": "", "css": "src/index.css", "baseColor": "zinc", "cssVariables": true, "prefix": "" }, "iconLibrary": "lucide", "aliases": { "components": "~/components", "utils": "~/lib/utils", "ui": "~/components/ui", "lib": "~/lib", "hooks": "~/hooks" } } ``` --- ### 6. TypeScript Conventions #### Import Style & Type Naming **From `/apps/web/src/types.ts` and throughout codebase**: **Naming Conventions**: - **Interfaces** for object shapes (rarely used, mostly types) - **Type Aliases** preferred for most definitions - **PascalCase** for all type names - **SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE** for constants **Examples**: ```tsx export type SessionPhase = "disconnected" | "connecting" | "ready" | "running"; export const DEFAULT_RUNTIME_MODE: RuntimeMode = "full-access"; export const DEFAULT_INTERACTION_MODE: ProviderInteractionMode = "default"; export interface ThreadTerminalGroup { id: string; terminalIds: string[]; } export interface ChatImageAttachment { type: "image"; id: string; name: string; mimeType: string; sizeBytes: number; previewUrl?: string; } export type ChatAttachment = ChatImageAttachment; export interface ChatMessage { id: MessageId; role: "user" | "assistant" | "system"; text: string; attachments?: ChatAttachment[]; createdAt: string; completedAt?: string | undefined; streaming: boolean; } ``` **Branded Types** (from Effect Schema): - Used for type-safe IDs: `ThreadId`, `ProjectId`, `MessageId`, etc. - Construction: `IdType.makeUnsafe(value)` or validation via Schema #### Store Types (Zustand) From `/apps/web/src/store.ts`: ```tsx export interface AppState { projects: Project[]; threads: Thread[]; threadsHydrated: boolean; } ``` #### React Component Types **Pattern**: Use `React.FC` or function components with typed props **Example Hook**: ```tsx export function useTheme() { const snapshot = useSyncExternalStore(subscribe, getSnapshot); return { theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme } as const; } ``` --- ### 7. Path Aliases (tsconfig) #### Root tsconfig.base.json Minimal configuration - extends ES2023, strict mode, moduleResolution "Bundler" #### apps/web/tsconfig.json ```json { "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json", "compilerOptions": { "composite": true, "jsx": "react-jsx", "lib": ["ES2023", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"], "types": ["vite/client"], "paths": { "~/*": ["./src/*"] }, "plugins": [ { "name": "@effect/language-service", "namespaceImportPackages": ["@effect/platform-node"], "diagnosticSeverity": { /* effects config */ } } ] }, "include": ["src", "vite.config.ts"] } ``` **Path Aliases**: - `~/` → `./src/` (all web app imports) - Also uses `~/*` pattern (e.g., `~/components`, `~/lib/utils`, `~/hooks`) --- ### 8. Global Window Declarations From `/apps/web/src/vite-env.d.ts`: ```tsx /// import type { NativeApi, DesktopBridge } from "@t3tools/contracts"; declare global { interface Window { nativeApi?: NativeApi; desktopBridge?: DesktopBridge; } } ``` **Purpose**: Adds type safety for Electron/desktop bridge and native API access --- ### 9. Vite Configuration Key features from `/apps/web/vite.config.ts`: ```tsx export default defineConfig({ plugins: [ tanstackRouter(), // Auto-generate route tree react({ babel: { plugins: [["babel-plugin-react-compiler", { target: "19" }]], }, }), tailwindcss(), // Tailwind CSS plugin ], optimizeDeps: { include: ["@pierre/diffs", "@pierre/diffs/react", "@pierre/diffs/worker/worker.js"], }, define: { "import.meta.env.VITE_WS_URL": JSON.stringify(process.env.VITE_WS_URL ?? ""), }, resolve: { tsconfigPaths: true, // Enable path aliases }, server: { port: 5733, // Default dev port hmr: { protocol: "ws", host: "localhost", }, }, build: { outDir: "dist", sourcemap: true, }, }); ``` --- ### 10. Providers & Context Pattern From `/apps/web/src/router.ts`: **Wrapping Pattern**: ```tsx return createRouter({ routeTree, history, context: { queryClient }, Wrap: ({ children }) => createElement( QueryClientProvider, { client: queryClient }, createElement(StoreProvider, null, children), ), }); ``` **Providers**: 1. **QueryClientProvider** (React Query) - Data fetching 2. **StoreProvider** (Zustand) - Global state 3. **ToastProvider** - Toast notifications (in root route) 4. **AnchoredToastProvider** - Anchored toast notifications **Composition Order**: QueryClient > Store > Children --- ### 11. State Management Pattern **Zustand Stores**: - `store.ts` - Main app state (projects, threads) - `composerDraftStore.ts` - Composer draft state (large file, 46KB) - `terminalStateStore.ts` - Terminal state (19KB) **Store Features**: - localStorage persistence with version keys - Event-driven updates from WebSocket - Legacy key cleanup - Query client integration for invalidation --- ### 12. Utility Functions From `/apps/web/src/lib/utils.ts`: ```tsx import { type CxOptions, cx } from "class-variance-authority"; import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge"; export function cn(...inputs: CxOptions) { return twMerge(cx(inputs)); } export function isMacPlatform(platform: string): boolean export function isWindowsPlatform(platform: string): boolean export const newCommandId = (): CommandId => CommandId.makeUnsafe(crypto.randomUUID()); export const newProjectId = (): ProjectId => ProjectId.makeUnsafe(crypto.randomUUID()); export const newThreadId = (): ThreadId => ThreadId.makeUnsafe(crypto.randomUUID()); export const newMessageId = (): MessageId => MessageId.makeUnsafe(crypto.randomUUID()); ``` --- ### Summary This is a sophisticated, well-structured **Electron + Web React monorepo** with: - **Strict Type Safety**: Effect Schema for branded IDs, TypeScript strict mode - **Modern React**: React 19, TanStack Router with auto-generated routes, TanStack Query - **State Management**: Zustand for global state with persistence - **Styling**: Tailwind CSS 4 with Base UI primitives and CVA - **Theme System**: Custom theme hook with light/dark/system modes - **Testing**: Vitest with browser testing support - **Shared Contracts**: Type-safe API contracts across apps - **Path Aliases**: `~/` for web app imports - **Effect-First**: Uses Effect library for functional programming and schema validation The architecture emphasizes **code reuse** through `@t3tools/contracts` and `@t3tools/shared`, **type safety** through branded types and strict TypeScript, and **modular styling** through Tailwind CSS with CSS custom properties for theming.

Everything looks clean. Here's a summary of all the changes made:

---

## Theme System Implementation

### Files Created (3 new files)

1. **`packages/shared/src/themes.ts`** — Shared theme definitions
   - `Theme` interface with `id`, `name`, `colors` (background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, statusBar), `fontFamily`, and `fontSize`
   - `ThemeColors` interface for the colour palette
   - 3 built-in themes: **T3 Dark** (default, dark neutral), **T3 Light** (clean white), **T3 Midnight** (deep blue)
   - `getTheme(id)` — look up a theme by id
   - `listThemes()` — return all built-in themes

2. **`apps/web/src/hooks/useAppTheme.tsx`** — React context + hook
   - `AppThemeProvider` — wraps the app, stores selected theme id in `localStorage` (`t3code:app-theme`), applies CSS custom properties (`--t3-theme-*`) to `<html>` on every theme change
   - Uses `useSyncExternalStore` for cross-tab sync (same pattern as the existing `useTheme` hook)
   - Applies properties on module load (flash prevention)
   - `useAppTheme()` hook returns `{ theme, setTheme, availableThemes }`

3. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — Visual theme picker
   - Radio-group of all available themes with colour swatch previews (shows background, accent, sidebar colours)
   - Matches the existing settings page styling patterns (border-primary, bg-primary/8, etc.)
   - "Active" badge on selected theme

### Files Modified (3 existing files)

4. **`packages/shared/package.json`** — Added `"./themes"` subpath export pointing to `src/themes.ts`

5. **`apps/web/src/router.ts`** — Wrapped the app with `AppThemeProvider` in the provider chain (QueryClient → **AppThemeProvider** → StoreProvider → children)

6. **`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Added a "Colour Theme" section between the existing Appearance and Codex App Server sections, using the `ThemeSelector` component and displaying the active palette name

### Verification
- **Lint**: 0 warnings, 0 errors on all new/modified files
- **Typecheck**: Passes cleanly (`npx tsc --noEmit`)
- **Tests**: All 315 tests pass across 35 test files
